mergeRationale: The guard short-circuits exactly the arange-with-zero-step crash for n_SV == 0, leaves the normal path untouched, and ships a faithful regression test. This mirrors the fix upstream merged for gh-14894.

requiredFindings.summary: Confirm the empty case crashed because dual_coef_indptr uses np.arange with step size/n_class == 0, and the n_SV == 0 guard short-circuits exactly that.
requiredFindings.expectedReasoning: When there are no support vectors dual_coef_indices is empty, so the arange step is 0 and raises; the new branch avoids building indptr at all.
requiredFindings.acceptableFix: No change needed. Optionally confirm with an SVR fit that yields zero support vectors.

requiredFindings.summary: Confirm the else branch preserves the original CSR construction, so non-empty fits are unaffected.
requiredFindings.expectedReasoning: The patch only adds a branch for n_SV == 0; the existing indptr/csr_matrix logic is moved verbatim into else.

requiredFindings.summary: Confirm the added test genuinely reproduces n_SV == 0 (linear SVR on the given inputs) and asserts the fix, rather than being a vacuous test.
requiredFindings.expectedReasoning: It fits an SVR that produces zero support vectors and asserts fit completes with empty support_vectors_ and dual_coef_.

optionalFindings.summary: A reviewer may ask whether the empty matrix should use an explicit shape, but the empty-support-vector contract here is that dual_coef_ has no coefficients; this is not grounds to reject.

disallowedConclusions: Request changes because sp.csr_matrix([]) does not force a particular two-dimensional empty shape.
Reject because the test only checks data.size and not dual_coef_.shape.
The empty case should raise instead of returning an empty matrix.
